blocks.content: February. Kit's mom asked him at dinner who he was taking to the Valentine's dance, which the high school did instead of a formal, and he said nobody.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: You went to the football games.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Football games are outside. Dances are—

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: Is there someone you want to take?
His mom cut a piece of chicken and didn't push.
He helped with the dishes afterward and she said, without turning from the sink:

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: You know you don't have to have everything figured out. You're fourteen.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: But if there's something you've been not saying to someone — and I think there might be — that kind of thing doesn't get easier the longer you wait.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You don't know what you're talking about.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: I know exactly what I'm talking about.

He went upstairs and opened his laptop and Daisy's MySpace showed the same song as last month. Ryan's profile had her in the first slot of his top eight. Kit looked at that for a long second. Then he closed the browser.
